reviews.review_text: It’s unbelievable that this game didn’t win any awards at The Game Awards. It’s an artistic and creative masterpiece. The amount of thought put into every single level is mind-blowing. The way it blends sci-fi and fantasy within the puzzle-platformer genre feels incredibly natural and coherent. In my opinion, this is the best puzzle-platformer I’ve ever played. Even at full price, this game is an absolute steal.

Dragged ourselves through Split Fiction.

Looks great, cool worlds and big cinematic set pieces, but the actual gameplay is extremely basic. Most of the time you’re just running forward, jumping, dashing, and occasionally using a special ability.

It feels more like a very long interactive movie than a real game. Barely any challenge, barely any skill involved, though some puzzles were actually pretty cool.

Probably good for non-gamers or people who just want a very simple co-op experience. But we play games pretty regularly, and for us it was honestly boring and underwhelming.

Also not sure who the target audience is: sometimes it feels like a kids’ game, then suddenly it has weirdly brutal moments.

Worth trying if it’s very cheap or free. A fair price for us would be around €5–10, but I wouldn’t recommend it at full price.

One of the best co-op game to ever exist. The studio oudid themselves. Its not a step up but 10 steps up over its previous entries. We have never witnessed this level of creativity and innovation in any game. A must try experiece, deserves to be purchased at full price.
I honestly don't understand how this game got overwhelmingly positive. The game play itself is great and very creative. But the story is so dang dumb.

This game is a simple yet mostly satisfying platformer with very pretty scenery (mainly in the fantasy bits though, some of the sci-fi levels can look very bland). But the enjoyment is sadly diminished by extremely unlikable characters who complain almost the whole game and a very simplistic and cartoonish story.

I still kinda liked it overall but the price I paid definitely leaves a sour taste... I would mostly recommend it if you get it for cheaper and/or if you share the price with the person you're playing with since you only need one copy for two people.

[u][b]Lifespan :[/b][/u] It took us 15 hours to finish the whole game then get all the achievements in chapter select.

[u][b]Story :[/b][/u] A big company called Rader Publishing invites writers to test a machine where they will live their story in a simulation. The playable characters are two of those writers: Zoe, a fantasy writer; and Mio, a sci-fi writer. When the machine starts and puts the writers into bubbles, Mio suddenly panics and wants out but the CEO stops her and she ends up falling into Zoe's simulation.
Now Zoe and Mio share the same simulation, which creates glitches. They are forced to learn to work together to go through the different worlds that regularly switch from fantasy to sci-fi. Their goal is trying to find those glitches so that it will shut down the machine and get them out of the simulation.
As they go, they learn that the company has hidden motives and wants more from the writers than just the one story they signed off.
During the game, you also slowly find out about Zoe's and Mio's pasts and what led them to become writers and come to this company to begin with.

This game's story was so annoying. Like I've said the playable characters complain non stop, which really takes you away from the wonder you should feel from exploring the different worlds.
The story with the company is just stupid. I'm gonna guess they signed a contract before getting there and they seem to put on the machine outfits with no complaining so why does Mio suddenly freaks out and thinks there's a conspiracy out of nowhere? At that point, there's really no reason to believe so. Then when she asks to leave, why does the CEO try to forcefully stop her? He has lots of willing writers and could bring more easily so why go to the trouble of stopping Mio? He could have also just calmly talked to her away from the machine to convince her to go back to it. It's just shown in such a ridiculous way that I instantly lost all investment in the story... and that was the very beginning of the game. And it doesn't get better, it gets more and more ridiculous as the game goes on. The CEO goes full on cartoon villain by the end.
Mio and Zoe also get backstories to make them more sympathetic but they're so annoying that by the time the backstory arrived, it was too late, I already couldn't care less about them. Zoe's story is a bit more endearing even if you could see it coming from a mile away. 

[u][b]Gameplay :[/b][/u]
For most chapters, you get a main world (sci-fi or fantasy) and then you can do optional side worlds of the opposite genre along the way.
Some mechanics stay the same but you get a new gimmick for each new main world and some of the side ones. For example in a world you can transform into creatures with certain abilities and in another you have dragons that can do things for you.
Zoe and Mio always have different abilities which forces them to work together to go through certain obstacles. I never felt like one got it better than the other. They always seemed pretty balanced and interesting in their own way.
From time to time, you will get boss fights where you will use the abilities of this world.
It's a shame that a lot of those worlds just felt like cool ideas for games and you only got to test a prototype.
It's also a very cinematic game and by that I mean you'll be running a lot. The platforming and puzzle aspects of the game are not that present sometimes.
As for the difficulty, it's mostly very simple. Some parts and boss fights might be a bit of a struggle but when you die, you can reappear after a few seconds as long as the other player doesn't also die at that moment so you can just brute force your way through it.

[u][b]Achievements :[/b][/u] The achievements are mostly for doing some silly things in levels. You also have an achievement for sitting on benches throughout the levels and one for doing all the optional side stories.
The only achievements you might struggle on are the two where you need to do something perfectly.
In any case, there is a chapter select so you can easily go back to the places where you missed an achievement or restart the parts where you need to do something perfectly until you get it right.
There is also a great guide on steam that will show you where all the achievements are and in story order.

Juicy game best of the three. overall a great improvement of it takes two. even know i like it takes two it   felt the game expecially at the end dragged on for the last 3 hours using the same excuse. for me the switch between sifi and fantasy fits a lot better. the story is the most emotional with trying to deal circumstances, trauma and cricumstance which i fin d more intreging and are more indepth than a relationship. i personally dont know what people are talking about saying this game has a shallow story when it takes two literally is a near one dimensional story.  the gameplay is general improvement it feels less of a platformer and more of it's own thing than it takes two. Rader is easly the best villan Hazelight has written as he feels very statisfying destroying that annoying ass.\

the side stories compared to the mini games is an execellent inclusion expecially how whacky they can get. 

Overall this game is a great improvement over it takes two. it takes all of previous stuff which makes split fiction the best game they have made.

If your going into this game expecting A Way Out, or It Takes Two then you might be disappointed. Those two previous games captured something that this one was not able to do. I cared about the characters in the first two games all the way through, in this game however i found myself wanting it to be over. I found the two protagonists to be a little annoying. Don't get me wrong, I didn't hate the game, i just don't think it is on the same level as the other two coop games before it. I feel like the puzzles and gameplay become repetitive fairly quickly and like i mentioned before the main characters aren't that likeable. It wasn't the worst game I've ever played and I can guarantee that if were both still able then me and my buddy will play the next one. I just want this to be a warning that if you are expecting Split Fiction to capture the magic of It Takes Two or A Way Out, it doesn't. But in the opinion of some random guy on the internet id say its worth a playthrough.

Highly recommed this game! 
First I played, It takes two then, A Way Out and lastly this, Split Fiction. 
Absolutely worth having all the three games to play with someone you know that has a galaxy wide of patience! 

Played all 3 games twice. Using friend's pass on the first playthrough. Bought all the games during sale time, bcoz I wanted to play it again but with a mission to get all the achievements. And so happy that I am able to, of course with the help of friends that has patience. 

I find it more fun and challenging doing the hunting achievements on this one, esp the [spoiler] Cold Potato and You Are Not A Robot (the capcha literally killed me) [/spoiler] . 

The shifting process between sci-fi and fantasy for me is so beautiful, like sci-fi are more into fast paced one, and its quite tricky to which is which to go next. While on fantasy is soothing and more of chillax one except for the [spoiler] flying kite one, thats so difficult like for real, real [/spoiler]
So its like, "ahh the stress of sci-fi is done for now, fantasy, here we go, yay! " 


Playing it again and recalling be like, "we did this on our first playthrough? I totally forgot." 🤣🤣🤣🤣

We was like, paused and look at the guide then continue again. Then laugh and have fun. 

Dont forget to always have fun while playing. Failing is the learning process. But in all honesty, I still have rusty reflexes on hands. 😂😂😂😂

Enjoy the game!

Excellent game 10/10. This game has the whimsy, the charm as well as the drama. The gameplay is excellent and the story is compelling. There were sad moments that got me and the game ends nicely with a satisfying conlcusion.   I can see why this game got game of the year nomination. I highly recommend this game!

I enjoyed the narrative of It Takes Two and A Way Out more, but the gameplay in Split Fiction is consistently inventive. Mechanically, it’s probably Hazelight’s most polished game. It constantly throws new ideas at the player before any one mechanic overstays its welcome. Still, the story never fully landed for me in the same way. While Split Fiction has a lot of imagination behind it, the constant shifts between worlds and concepts sometimes made the experience feel less cohesive. This aspect was very entertaining from a gameplay perspective, but hurt the narrative a bit. Additionally, the characters left a lot to be desired for me personally. I still recommend it purely for the gameplay.

A Way Out was a master piece
It Takes Two was good
Split Fiction is... very dissapointing

It's like it's either made for millennials or people who don't play video games

The story is just very boring  and predictable as well as the dialogue
The humor is just, not there.

Sure the gameplay is okay at best but not punishing at all and holds your hand a lot
and the graphics are beautiful

But a game that is so heavily focused on being more like a movie and story based it's extremely lacking especially  compared to its predecessor

Your mom might have fun playing this
Just finished playing this with my partner, and we absolutely loved it! It’s such a beautiful, charming, and heartwarming game. Highly recommended if you want a great co-op experience!

Split Fiction somehow manages to throw a new idea at you every 10 minutes and almost all of them work. fantastic co-op gameplay, creative level design, and enough variety to keep things fresh from start to finish. easy recommendation if you have a friend to play with.

I've completed it twice already, and honestly, I had an amazing time on both playthroughs. I think Hazelight has truly perfected its co-op formula. especially the way split screen gameplay becomes more than just a mechanic and actually feels like part of the story itself. That level of integration is genuinely impressive. Both it takes two and Split Fiction felt incredibly original to me. I couldn't say one is better than the other because each has its own charm, but Split Fiction definitely made me laugh a lot more. It's an excellent game to play with either a partner or friends. While it's relatively short, every moment feels worthwhile, and it's absolutely worth every penny:
Amazing Story top game I've played in a minute! The next game I'm sure will be amazing especially playing the other 2!

Abssolutely loved the game! The game is insanely creative, fun, emotional, and packed with amazing co-op moments from start to finish. Every chapter felt fresh and unique, and the gameplay variety kept surprising us constantly. The visuals, storytelling, and character chemistry were all top tier.

I also felt like a perfect continuation of what made It Takes Two so special- Taking the same magical co-op experience and making it even bigger and more imaginative. Easily one of the best co-op games I've played. Highly recommended!

its fun, but hella cringy and they should have been gay. it was obviously an enemies to lovers type beat but then (trauma flashbacks to stranger things and byler) they just queer bated the ♥♥♥♥ out of us with no lovers anywhere to be found.
So far, this is my favorite Hazelight game. I recently played through the first two, and you can tell the studio gets more experienced, creative and better with each game, culminating in this gem.
Super fun gameplay with some really creative ideas. The main plot not as engaging as in the previous game but it did not detract from the game much. Highly recommended!

Beautiful game, innovative gameplay, constantly fresh and entertaining.

You don't find many good co-op experiences like these.

Feels especially good faith from the devs that they only require you to buy one copy to play with a friend.

The game is very enjoyable.

However I have to say that I'm somewhat disappointed at the low difficulty - I am NOT saying the game should be very challenging - I'm just stating, this game is easy. And the fact that there are many segments where we are just split-screened and nothing is happening. One moment like a real co-op one is e.g. riding and the other is aiming, but next segment we're each on our own again just running around? I expected "okay this level I have this mechanic, you have that one, we get a practice puzzle and off to the challenge!"... Yes but not really.

That being said I have to applaud the developers for easter eggs! The gameplay and the story were enjoyable. Co-op has its ups and downs, but I would play it again though! Looking forward to the third installment!

Split Fiction is a very fun experience overall. I really enjoyed the variety of ideas throughout the game, and visually it looks stunning from start to finish.

However, one downside is that some of the concepts are really interesting but not fully explored. A few mechanics or ideas are introduced only once and then never used again, which made them feel a bit underused.

Despite that, it’s still a great and enjoyable game worth playing.
It's a great game. The only issue is that each chapter has a different art style, which makes the overall experience feel somewhat disjointed. And overall story line is not good as it takes two.

Split fiction is honestly the most overrated game I have ever played and I’m shocked it’s overwhelming positive on steam.

I loved It Takes Two and A Way Out so it was such a shock to me that this game was a drag to get through, I was so hyped to play it when it got announced. However the writing, uninspired levels and characters just made this soo bleh.

The writing is the worse thing about this game, so if you’re looking for just a game for 2 player coop gameplay and don’t care about story at all, then this may be for you, but considering there’s so many other great choices with good writing this one falls flat. The dialogue in this game is just horrid as the main characters constantly shout out one outliers and when they’re not doing that you have to push through an obnoxiously optimistic character and a cringy edgy character. Throughout the entire game my friend and I were just groaning to the cringy dialogue and forced “you can talk to me, I’m here for you” moments, which sucks because I was really trying to get into the story, it’s just so painfully meh.

Not to mention the story is just boring / very obvious, you know how the story will end because the villain is very 1 dimensional. I did like how they tried to put a semi serious story segment near the end, but honestly I couldn’t care less by that point.

The idea of the game is cool, I like the sci-fi mix with fantasy, and honestly they have a really cool final level that mixes the two well, but most of the games levels are really boring and generic fantasy and sci-fi settings. The side stories are really cool however, they feel really creative and it feels like there’s passion put into them, it just sucks that they’re so small and the main levels are boring / very generic.

Another positive is the gameplay is mostly fun, if not repetitive. It feels like they just kinda took the gameplay of it takes two and redid it, which is not a bad thing I guess, just gets a bit repetitive.

Overall this game is just a mess and felt like a huge disappointment to me. The gameplay isn’t anything unique and done way better in their last game, and the writing is just so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ bad. I had to drag myself through this game to finish it and don’t feel satisfied completing it. The only good parts of this game are the side, optional and missable stories, which are short and fun, but few and far between. I do NOT recommend, spend like 5 bucks on Lego Batman 1 you’ll have a way better time.


TLDR: this game is an overrated mess with horrid writing, you’re better off skipping it.

surprisingly good re play value
good co op
deserved more recognition, just got released in a year of absolute bangers

7 hours in, and it seems like there is a ways to go. I am delighted by this one. I think I prefer the scifi side of things for the first part, but later it got better. I really think this was a great idea, and hope for more stories. More fun mini game comps too!

The villain is so horrendously cliche and every line of dialogue feels like a first draft. Really disapointed because I enjoyed It Takes Two, and I LOVED A Way Out. It's ironic a story about writers is so poorly written. It's very unclear if these are novel authors, screenplay writers, or game writers? They just keep saying "writer" over and over again and refer to their work as "stories" instead of saying "book" or "film script". It sounds very unnatural. I wish there was more diversity in the sci-fi worlds. Most of them are just space, but sci-fi can be so much more than that! Like how Manware is more grounded on earth but everyone has robotic limbs and the technology is more advanced. The villain is just a super flat evil tech CEO. I wish he was at least funny or had SOME kind of interesting personality. I will say the gameplay can be fun at some parts, but the puzzles are so overly simple. It feels more like the game is a shallow rollercoaster ride than a good narrative or fun in depth game. This just feels like a step down from the studio's previous games.
Amazing game. A lot of thought and creativity went into making this game.

Excellent game.

- Now we just need an MMO style RPG with this concept crossed with Tad Williams "Otherland" book series.
Great Co-play integrations with constant evolution of game mechanics.  A top five game of the last ten years of game play.

Probably one of the most epic adventures I've ever been on - Split Fiction is creative and unique as it explores different gaming genres and styles while staying true to its roots and storyline. My sister and I had many moments of frustration (with lots of shouting & swearing) and laughter too; whilst being thousands of miles apart from each other! 

But all in all, we were taken to another world where fantasy and science fiction converge, and in this case, teamwork really makes the dream work. With 35 hours of gameplay in a single playthrough, you can switch sides and replay everything over and over again. Highly recommended for those looking for something different to play with friends or family.
Had a lot of fun playing the game. Has nice visuals and a good story. Will play it again.
This is by far the best co-op game out there.  So much fun and challenging at the same time.  Keep it up!

Apart from almost one hour during the whole game, the remaining is utterly boring with repetitive mechanics. The game is basically a lot of parkour and the story is not engaging. It takes two was a lot better. It is definitely not worth its full price.
So so fun. The gameplay variety, the pop culture references and the meta theme really pops out in this game the best way possible. One of the best gaming experiences I've ever had.
Amazing game ,we really loed it with my girlfriend . Be carefeul if you overclocked your RAM it might cause some issues

Masterpiece.

Cool side mission and easter eggs.

Has some very trippy parts.

Extremely beautiful.

This has genuinely been one of the most entertaining games I’ve played in a long time. The story was so heartfelt, I legitimately teared up multiple times. The visuals were breathtaking and the gameplay extremely satisfying and fun. 10/10

The game is AMAZING! Played and beat it with my daughter and we loved it.  Going to play it again using opposite characters than we did the first go round before going after the other games by this studio.  Split fiction...my favorite Co-op game so far!

Great to play as a couple or with a friend! Excellent split-screen experience. A great story with beautiful scenery, environments, music, and just a great experience. We loved the switching between sci-fi and fantasy. Would recommend this to anyone. [spoiler]It gets wild.[/spoiler] 10.0/10

Infinitely creative, funny, and engaging. One of the best co-op games ever made. We played on an ultrawide monitor and it was a fantastic experience. 

The dialogue between the two protagonists is a little repetitive in the beginning, but the characters become less one-note towards the later third of the game.
An incredible game. Brilliant designs, graphics, and gameplay.

loved jumping between worlds and kicking butt in both sci-fi and fantasy worlds. Had an absolute blast playing with my girlfriend and wish i could forget it all so we could play it all over again like it was the first time. 10/10 amazing game with my amazing girl

Absolutely amazing experience - the creativity, innovation around splitscreen gameplay, the references/hommage to other games is stunning. I can't describe it, I am overwhelmed by all the individual scenes and puzzles and bosses... we will instantly make another playthrough but swapping roles.
This has to be one of my favorite games of all time. It's like playing a different game every couple of minutes and the references are numerous and fantastic. Love this game.
Another masterpiece. I played it with my girlfriend and loved every minute of it.

Story was great and gameplay felt rewarding
Awesome gameplay, storyline, graphics & audio design. Everything is just perfect for me!

Gameplay was okay at best and the story was ATROCIOUS.

Like genuinely unlikeable, Mio is so unlikeable, the dialogue is boring and predictable.

Final boss fight was HORRENDOUS. Had so much higher hopes going into this from A Way Out and It Takes Two.

Peak gameplay, felt so satisfying to play with my friend after playing mainly competitive / solo adventure games. Our time was filled with laughter, betrayal, and sadness thank you for this experience
Super fun couch game to play with someone who is less gaming inclined. Regularly changes up the genre to keep things fresh and the ending is truly unique.

The imagination on this developer is genuinely insane. Amazing.

I've played all of Hazelights games and they need to be protected at all costs.

As someone who has played It Takes Two, coming to Split Fiction feels like an ode to what the developers really wanted to do- and then cranked that sht up to the max.

The blend of the 2 most grossing fiction genres was so effortless and seamless that it split my head open in ways I didn't even know could be split.

Difficulty was challenging but not impossible, average gamers can nail it down in a few tries.

Story was incredible, intertwining 2 stories from 2 extremely different characters in a way that came together seamlessly in the end.

10/10 would play it again !!
One of the best games I've ever played. The amount of variety they included in the game is really commendable.

An astoundingly inventive and passionate game. 15 straight hours of pure innovation, managing to not get dull for a single second - instead continuously presenting you with something new and fun to do every few minutes. It genuinely only evolves its concepts over the course of the entire game and manages to finish on a high note on top of that. The story is simple, but well presented and ended up being surprisingly touching. Looks beautiful and provides stunning vistas while running like butter, too.

I'm eagerly awaiting Hazelight's next game, I can't wait to see what they do next. Through their hard effort, they've managed to create a formula of pure distilled joy that I honestly haven't seen replicated elsewhere. I'm glad they're being rewarded for it.
This game is amazing! The developers put so much love and care into their stories and this is no exception. 10 out of 10!
The game was a fun co-op experience, with a great story, and many diverse gameplay experience!

This is my GOTY. Nice visual and world. It's gets action packed right from the start and keeps going forward hard! I can't seem to find enough people to replay this with even though it has a friend's pass! It's very replayable! I enjoy seeing other people's reaction with this game. Best co-op. Game runs very well with my low configuration laptop. Even runs well on my Legion Go S.

Only played an hour of this game and it's already a favourite. I was looking for a co-op game to play with my kid and this one doesn't disappoint. The story is really compelling and the game play is great. 10/10

This game was absolutely incredible. We played through it all in 1 sitting. It was such a wild ride and there are so many parts that we would play entire games of for just those sections. Just another absolute banger from this development team. We did manage to find some bugs from our incredible knack for being terrible at games but I would play this again in an instant... Maybe not an instant... I don't have another 16 hours to spend on a single game straight lol

This has been the ABSOLUTE best game i have ever played as a co-op game, it was beautiful! funny! sad! and just completely had me hooked and in AWE the whole entire time. I cant wait to see what else may come from this team. LOVE LOVE LOVE IT. Highly recommend anyone that has a bestie or a family member or just anyone to hop on this one together and enjoy it to the fullest!
Awesome game, lots of fun. Story is less cringe than It Takes Two. Good graphics. Last level is short but really fun.

While this is a good game, it's far from perfect. The banter between characters often feels clunky, and levels have patterns of similarity between each-other that makes the gameplay start to feel stale during late game sections. The emotional parts of the narrative tend to feel a bit forced, but a few moments did feel quite genuine. Some of the set pieces were stunning, but it was jarring how some assets in a level had low resolution textures and strange hitboxes. Many parts of the game allow one player to run far ahead without cooperating, I think I would appreciate some more sections that required input from both players to progress. 

I do think the game did a good job of giving each story a unique visual flair. There was always something interesting to look at, but I would have liked to see some more interactive elements to break up the linear gameplay. I am glad they had sidestories to complete - I found each one enjoyable. Overall, there were some drab moments, but the majority of my time in the game was fun.
Very good successor to "It takes two" and very fun to play co-op. 10/10

Its taken over a year to complete this game with myself and my brother playing.  My brother on a PS5 and me on a host PC.  Its taken this long mainly due to us both being available and my brother working nights but we both really enjoyed playing through this.  I've always prefered co-op multiplayer to vs and this really plays to that strength.  Graphics are excellent and the story while a little cliche in places works and your always wanting to see what madness is next.  The later half of the game that really then plays to the games name is a particular highlight and it really messes with your perception with the split screen.  The cross platform compatibility works really well and generally never had any issues with it - maybe one crash.  Overall its a fantastic co-op game that you should play with your other half, friend, sibling and you'll have a great time.
Incredible game. Played with wife non gamer. and it was so much fun.
Possibly the most incredible game I have ever played.  The creativity is over the top.  The 2 player gameplay is just freaking amazing.  LOVE this game.  Should be game of the year without question.
Played It takes two previously. This tops it immensly. Loved basically everything about it. It just gets better and better the further into the game you go. Just get sucked in
The scenery is amazing. The gameplay is a bit hard for my wife but we're making it through it.

Split Fiction has some of the best video game being a video game moments I've ever experienced. It's incredibly fun and the story is full of heart. I really hope Hazelight keeps making games like this and It Takes Two, they're absolute treasures.

This game focuses FAR more on the genre hopping and homages to games you may or may not have played before, than it does the co-operative aspects. At least 50% of our (me and my boyfriend) playtime was spent doing the same thing as one another. SOME CO-OP PARTS WERE GOOD! The good bits were few and far between, as were the co-op sections in general.
Genre hopping meant it did several genres but didn't focus on any of them so they were mostly unimpressive and lacked any depth.

There are about 1800 hours worth of cutscenes with random leaps in logic that the characters make, given the information presented to them (they quite often act based on information WE THE PLAYERS know but THEY THE CHARACTERS have no way of knowing). The theme is fine, the dialogue is horrible, the acting is fine. We ended up skipping most of the cutscenes after a while because it was just like Johnny's first script levels of script.
PLAY IT TAKES TWO INSTEAD! (I hear it's actually focused on the co-op)
Wow, just wow. This game is an experience not just a game, I haven't felt like a child while playing a game for a long time.

Insanely good, best multiplayer game I have ever played. Has aspects of every game, and kept surprising me with all the different game mechanics it kept introducing... So much content extremely worth it.

This game left me and my wife so awestruck and attached that we watched through the credits just to appreciate all the people and their hard work that made this game the way it is. The level design, ingenuity and creativity in mechanics and perspectives is rivaled by nothing else we have ever played. The forced perspectives of the final levels created my favorite sequence in gaming ever. Absolutely cannot recommend enough.

To no ones surprise this game is awesome!

If you liked this studios previous game It Takes Two you'll love Split fiction.

In it's essence it is very similar: It's a well functioning co-op platformer with an incredible amount of creativity poured into it.
The leves, themes, mechanics (especially the co-op mechanics) vary so much and in so many super fun ways!

If you like co-op games in general, give it a try!
If you like co-op platformers you'll love it!
And if you like platformers but have nobody to play this with, then find one and play it!

29 hours of diverse gameplay, stunning graphics, and creative mechanics. Hazelight raises the bar they set with It Takes Two, showing exactly how cooperative games should be made. By the end, you'll be amazed by the developers' skills themselves — blending different realities and gameplay styles on one screen is not sth trivial at all. 10/10.
Excellent graphics and storyline, would strongly suggest playing!!
Great coop game super fun if you like it takes two runs perfectly.

absolute cinema. this game is fantastic and the final hour of this game is genuinely unlike anything i have seen in a video game. completely unmatched and this game feels way longer than the playtime in a good way. 10/10
great Co-op game, reminds me over split-screen feature back in the day .
It's a very unique game I would say... It's really fun! I enjoy the fantasy and si-fi aspect of the game, the animations are really good.

This game was great! If you liked It Takes Two, this game is more of that Hazelight magic. Uniquely two player levels, constantly evolving mechanics, boss-fights that are memorable and varied, and a million little nods, buried references, and humor points to make the game shine! The story was not quite as tight as the It Takes Two, imo, but it was still fun. I loved the game! The side stories were super creative and the last level was something out of a fever dream in the BEST way. Finally published!
Great game and great platforming, decent optimization and a really nice runtime, great to play through with a friend
really good! they went crazy on the level design in the last hour or so of the story, and the mechanics & puzzles in the rest of the game are fantastic as usual from hazelight :)

The creativity of the two types of worlds in this game was amazing. I also loved how you learned deeper about each character and the development they went through. The game had lots of exciting plots and fun obstacles and boss battles! The end was especially amazing, you gotta play to see how cool it is!

My partner and I loved this game. We liked the originality of the story concepts and side stories. My partner said this is probably the most fun platformer they've played and I agree. The story is compelling, the abilities are intuitive and forgiving, and you can really laugh at the silliness of missing a grapple or something. We would play every game they make for the next 20 years probably.

This was an incredible game. Amazing visuals. Great playthrough and puzzle game. 10/10 would recommed again and again. Thank you for not making us have to purchase it twice. I will buy any game you put out. Thank you!

One of the best co-op games I’ve played in a while aside from It Takes Two. Every level feels unique and keeps the game fresh and exciting. The teamwork mechanics are really fun and make playing with a friend even better. The bonus level is incredibly hard... that being said I'll beat it eventually lol
This game trained my 5yo daughter on the grown-up controller. It's amazing. Finally completed it this morning and now we're achievement hunting. Well done, triple-A co-op game.

The best co-op  game  <3
- Gameplay
- Visual
- Gameplay
- Sound
- Gameplay
- Story
- GAMEPLAY!!!11
Incredibly fun and technically a master piece. Each level is essentially a new game mode :O.
amazing game with briliant strory and gameplay

16 hours of gameplay and a ton of fun! Played with my husband, I think it's a good balance between challenging yet easy enough to feel rewarding when it needs to be. Playing on key board is a little bit of a challenge so if you have a controller it's reccomended to play with that. Gotta love some of the nods to older games or pop culture, and while challenging at times I absolutely love the use of different screen style to incorporate a variety of gameplay types. I've played both it takes two and now split fiction with my husband, and we've both had an amazing time with them.

Game is worth to play, but it is very different than It Takes Two. 

Grab your friend/love and play it!
Great two-player game, even for someone who's terrible on a controller. It can be played using keyboard for most of the game.

Great couples co-op game. The unique gameplay mechanic they introduce right near the end is going to be making a comeback in a sequel for sure. It's awesome to see innovative gameplay so well done. You're also going to be cutting onions near the end, so be prepared.

Very fun, better than It Takes Two on pretty much every aspect.
But it's a lot more challenging. It Takes Two was a nice game to play with someone who never played video games, this is not the case here. Seasoned gamers won't have any trouble though.

Most beautiful experience of Visuals, Concept, Gameplay and Dying.
Harmful amd beautiful for both eyes.
Playing through with my 7 year old son and having a blast even if it might take a bit longer than usual. The creativity, stories, and mechanics have been quite unique and fun!
